bpy.ops.mesh.attribute_set(value_float=0.0,value_float_vector_2d=(0.0,0.0),value_float_vector_3d=(0.0,0.0,0.0),value_int=0,value_int_vector_2d=(0,0),value_color=(1.0,1.0,1.0,1.0),value_bool=False)#

Set values of the active attribute for selected elementsParameters:value_float(float in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Valuevalue_float_vector_2d(float arrayof2 items in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Valuevalue_float_vector_3d(float arrayof3 items in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Valuevalue_int(int in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Valuevalue_int_vector_2d(int arrayof2 items in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Valuevalue_color(float arrayof4 items in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Valuevalue_bool(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Value

bpy.ops.mesh.average_normals(average_type='CUSTOM_NORMAL',weight=50,threshold=0.01)#

Average custom normals of selected verticesParameters:average_type(enum in['CUSTOM_NORMAL','FACE_AREA','CORNER_ANGLE'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Type, Averaging methodCUSTOM_NORMALCustom Normal ГўВҖВ“ Take average of vertex normals.FACE_AREAFace Area ГўВҖВ“ Set all vertex normals by face area.CORNER_ANGLECorner Angle ГўВҖВ“ Set all vertex normals by corner angle.weight(int in[1,100],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Weight, Weight applied per facethreshold(float in[0,10],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Threshold, Threshold value for different weights to be considered equal

bpy.ops.mesh.beautify_fill(angle_limit=3.14159)#

Rearrange some faces to try to get less degenerated geometryParameters:angle_limit(float in[0,3.14159],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Max Angle, Angle limit

bpy.ops.mesh.bevel(offset_type='OFFSET',offset=0.0,profile_type='SUPERELLIPSE',offset_pct=0.0,segments=1,profile=0.5,affect='EDGES',clamp_overlap=False,loop_slide=True,mark_seam=False,mark_sharp=False,material=-1,harden_normals=False,face_strength_mode='NONE',miter_outer='SHARP',miter_inner='SHARP',spread=0.1,vmesh_method='ADJ',release_confirm=False)#

Cut into selected items at an angle to create bevel or chamferParameters:offset_type(enum in['OFFSET','WIDTH','DEPTH','PERCENT','ABSOLUTE'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Width Type, The method for determining the size of the bevelOFFSETOffset ГўВҖВ“ Amount is offset of new edges from original.WIDTHWidth ГўВҖВ“ Amount is width of new face.DEPTHDepth ГўВҖВ“ Amount is perpendicular distance from original edge to bevel face.PERCENTPercent ГўВҖВ“ Amount is percent of adjacent edge length.ABSOLUTEAbsolute ГўВҖВ“ Amount is absolute distance along adjacent edge.offset(float in[0,1e+06],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Width, Bevel amountprofile_type(enum in['SUPERELLIPSE','CUSTOM'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Profile Type, The type of shape used to rebuild a beveled sectionSUPERELLIPSESuperellipse ГўВҖВ“ The profile can be a concave or convex curve.CUSTOMCustom ГўВҖВ“ The profile can be any arbitrary path between its endpoints.offset_pct(float in[0,100],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Width Percent, Bevel amount for percentage methodsegments(int in[1,1000],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Segments, Segments for curved edgeprofile(float in[0,1],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Profile, Controls profile shape (0.5 = round)affect(enum in['VERTICES','EDGES'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Affect, Affect edges or verticesVERTICESVertices ГўВҖВ“ Affect only vertices.EDGESEdges ГўВҖВ“ Affect only edges.clamp_overlap(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Clamp Overlap, Do not allow beveled edges/vertices to overlap each otherloop_slide(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Loop Slide, Prefer sliding along edges to even widthsmark_seam(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Mark Seams, Mark Seams along beveled edgesmark_sharp(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Mark Sharp, Mark beveled edges as sharpmaterial(int in[-1,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Material Index, Material for bevel faces (-1 means use adjacent faces)harden_normals(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Harden Normals, Match normals of new faces to adjacent facesface_strength_mode(enum in['NONE','NEW','AFFECTED','ALL'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Face Strength Mode, Whether to set face strength, and which faces to set face strength onNONENone ГўВҖВ“ Do not set face strength.NEWNew ГўВҖВ“ Set face strength on new faces only.AFFECTEDAffected ГўВҖВ“ Set face strength on new and modified faces only.ALLAll ГўВҖВ“ Set face strength on all faces.miter_outer(enum in['SHARP','PATCH','ARC'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Outer Miter, Pattern to use for outside of mitersSHARPSharp ГўВҖВ“ Outside of miter is sharp.PATCHPatch ГўВҖВ“ Outside of miter is squared-off patch.ARCArc ГўВҖВ“ Outside of miter is arc.miter_inner(enum in['SHARP','ARC'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Inner Miter, Pattern to use for inside of mitersSHARPSharp ГўВҖВ“ Inside of miter is sharp.ARCArc ГўВҖВ“ Inside of miter is arc.spread(float in[0,1e+06],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Spread, Amount to spread arcs for arc inner mitersvmesh_method(enum in['ADJ','CUTOFF'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Vertex Mesh Method, The method to use to create meshes at intersectionsADJGrid Fill ГўВҖВ“ Default patterned fill.CUTOFFCutoff ГўВҖВ“ A cutoff at each profileГўВҖВҷs end before the intersection.release_confirm(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Confirm on Release

bpy.ops.mesh.bisect(plane_co=(0.0,0.0,0.0),plane_no=(0.0,0.0,0.0),use_fill=False,clear_inner=False,clear_outer=False,threshold=0.0001,xstart=0,xend=0,ystart=0,yend=0,flip=False,cursor=5)#

Cut geometry along a plane (click-drag to define plane)Parameters:plane_co(mathutils.Vectorof 3 items in [-inf, inf], (optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Plane Point, A point on the planeplane_no(mathutils.Vectorof 3 items in [-1, 1], (optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Plane Normal, The direction the plane pointsuse_fill(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Fill, Fill in the cutclear_inner(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Clear Inner, Remove geometry behind the planeclear_outer(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Clear Outer, Remove geometry in front of the planethreshold(float in[0,10],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Axis Threshold, Preserves the existing geometry along the cut planexstart(int in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ X Startxend(int in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ X Endystart(int in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Y Startyend(int in[-inf,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Y Endflip(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Flipcursor(int in[0,inf],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Cursor, Mouse cursor style to use during the modal operator

in['VGROUP_WEIGHTS','BEVEL_WEIGHT_VERT','COLOR_VERTEX','SHARP_EDGE','SEAM','CREASE','BEVEL_WEIGHT_EDGE','FREESTYLE_EDGE','CUSTOM_NORMAL','COLOR_CORNER','UV','SMOOTH','FREESTYLE_FACE'],(optional)) ГўВҖВ“Data Type, Which data to transferVGROUP_WEIGHTSVertex Group(s) ГўВҖВ“ Transfer active or all vertex groups.BEVEL_WEIGHT_VERTBevel Weight ГўВҖВ“ Transfer bevel weights.COLOR_VERTEXColors ГўВҖВ“ Color Attributes.SHARP_EDGESharp ГўВҖВ“ Transfer sharp mark.SEAMUV Seam ГўВҖВ“ Transfer UV seam mark.CREASESubdivision Crease ГўВҖВ“ Transfer crease values.BEVEL_WEIGHT_EDGEBevel Weight ГўВҖВ“ ГўВҖВ“ Transfer UV layers.SMOOTHSmooth ГўВҖВ“ Transfer flat/smooth mark.FREESTYLE_FACEFreestyle Mark ГўВҖВ“ Transfer Freestyle face mark.use_create(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Create Data, Add data onesuse_auto_transform(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Auto Transform, Automatically compute transformation to get the best possible match between source and destination meshes.Warning: Results will never be as good as manual matching of objectsuse_object_transform(boolean,(optional)) ГўВҖВ“ Object Transform, Evaluate source and destination meshes in global spaceuse_max_distance(boolean,(optional)) Example:
This script demonstrates basic operations on object like creating new
object, placing it into a view layer, selecting it and making it active.

```python
importbpyview_layer=bpy.context.view_layer# Create new light datablock.light_data=bpy.data.lights.new(name="New Light",type='POINT')# Create new object with our light datablock.light_object=bpy.data.objects.new(name="New Light",object_data=light_data)# Link light object to the active collection of current view layer,# so that it'll appear in the current scene.view_layer.active_layer_collection.collection.objects.link(light_object)# Place light to a specified location.light_object.location=(5.0,5.0,5.0)# And finally select it and make it active.light_object.select_set(True)view_layer.objects.active=light_object
```

Example:
This example script prints the vertices and UVs for each polygon, assumes the active object is a mesh with UVs.

```python
importbpyme=bpy.context.object.datauv_layer=me.uv_layers.active.dataforpolyinme.polygons:print("Polygon index:{:d}, length:{:d}".format(poly.index,poly.loop_total))# range is used here to show how the polygons reference loops,# for convenience 'poly.loop_indices' can be used instead.forloop_indexinrange(poly.loop_start,poly.loop_start+poly.loop_total):print("    Vertex:{:d}".format(me.loops[loop_index].vertex_index))print("    UV:{!r}".format(uv_layer[loop_index].uv))
```

Type:(bpy.types.AnyType,string,int)Get the property associated with a hovered button.
Returns a tuple of the data-block, data path to the property, and array index.NoteWhen the property doesnГўВҖВҷt have an associatedbpy.types.IDnon-ID data may be returned.
This may occur when accessing windowing data, for example, operator properties.# Example inserting keyframe for the hovered property.active_property=bpy.context.propertyifactive_property:datablock,data_path,index=active_propertydatablock.keyframe_insert(data_path=data_path,index=index,frame=1)

bpy.data.data#

Access to BlenderГўВҖВҷs internal dataType:bpy.types.BlendData

Example:
bpy.types.BlendData

```python
importbpy# print all objectsforobjinbpy.data.objects:print(obj.name)# print all scene names in a listprint(bpy.data.scenes.keys())# remove mesh Cubeif"Cube"inbpy.data.meshes:mesh=bpy.data.meshes["Cube"]print("removing mesh",mesh)bpy.data.meshes.remove(mesh)# write images into a file next to the blendimportoswithopen(os.path.splitext(bpy.data.filepath)[0]+".txt",'w')asfs:forimageinbpy.data.images:fs.write("{:s}{:d}x{:d}\n".format(image.filepath,image.size[0],image.size[1]))
```
